title: Questions over safety as 16 pupils die in another Kenya school fire
content: Several parents were still waiting for news of their children roughly 12 hours after the fire.

title: First Ghanaians evacuated from South Africa over immigration protests land in Accra
content: It follows recent protests that have led to fears there could be a resurgence in xenophobic violence.

originalTitle: Ebola-hit DR Congo faces 'catastrophic collision' of disease and conflict, WHO warns
title: Big News from Africa: Helping Friends in DR Congo!
content:
Hello everyone! Your friendly news reporter from Africa is here! Today, we're going to talk about some very brave people and a special country called DR Congo. It's a wonderful place in the heart of Africa, but right now, some friends there need our thoughts and help.

Meet Our Friends in DR Congo!

DR Congo is a really big country. It's in the middle of Africa, and it has lots of amazing things. Imagine huge, green forests, big rivers, and all sorts of cool animals! Many people live there, and kids just like you love to play games and learn. **Did you know?** DR Congo is the second-largest country in all of Africa! That's bigger than many countries put together, almost like a giant adventure park! It's a beautiful place with many kind people, and it's full of life.

What is Ebola? It's a Sneaky Sickness.

Sometimes, people get sick. You know, like when you have a cold or a tummy ache. Most times, it's not too bad, and you get better quickly. But in DR Congo, there is a sickness called Ebola. It's a very, very strong sickness. It's like having a super bad flu that makes people feel very, very tired and hot. Ebola can make people feel very sick, very quickly. It's caused by a tiny germ. Germs are like tiny, tiny little things that we cannot see with our eyes. **Did you know?** Washing your hands with soap and clean water helps chase away many germs, including some that can make you sick! It’s a super simple way to stay healthy! This sickness can spread from one person to another if they are not careful.

Super Helpers Are There!

When people get sick, we need helpers, right? Like doctors and nurses who know how to make people better. In DR Congo, there are many doctors and nurses. They are like real-life superheroes! They wear special protective suits to stay safe while helping people. They give medicine and care to people who have Ebola. They work super hard, day and night, to help everyone get well again. There's a very important group called WHO. It stands for the World Health Organization. WHO is like a big team of health helpers for the whole world! They send even more doctors, nurses, and special equipment to places like DR Congo. Their job is to help countries stop sickness from spreading. They teach people how to stay safe and how to make sure others don't get sick too. They are working very hard to help our friends in DR Congo.

A Big Problem: The Fighting.

Now, here is something sad. In some parts of DR Congo, there is fighting. It's not like kids arguing over a toy; it's grown-ups fighting with weapons. When there is fighting, it makes everything very, very difficult. People can get hurt, and they often have to leave their homes to find a safer place to live. Roads can get blocked or become dangerous to travel on. Stores might close, and it can be hard to get food or other important things. This fighting makes things very tough for everyone in the area. It causes a lot of worry and sadness.

Why Fighting Makes Helping Hard.

Imagine you are one of those brave doctors. You want to go help a sick person, but the roads are blocked, or there is fighting nearby. It's just not safe to go. Or maybe, because of the fighting, people have run away from their homes. It becomes very hard for the doctors and nurses to find them and give them help. Sometimes, the people fighting even stop the helpers from doing their job. They might scare them away or not let them bring medicine to the people who need it most. The WHO says this fighting is making it much, much harder to stop Ebola. It's like two big, bad problems are crashing into each other. They call it a "catastrophic collision." This big phrase just means that the sickness (Ebola) and the fighting are happening at the same time, making everything much worse and harder to fix. It means that more people might get sick because the helpers can't reach them. It's a very serious problem that makes everyone worried.

But We Have Hope and Hard Workers!

Even with all the fighting and the sickness, the helpers are not giving up! They are so brave and strong. The doctors, nurses, and the WHO teams are working very hard. They are trying their very best to reach everyone who needs help. They keep teaching people how to stay safe. They tell everyone, "Wash your hands!" and "Stay away from sick people if you can!" These are important rules to stop germs. They are also talking to the local leaders, trying to find ways to make the areas safer so they can do their important work. **Did you know?** When everyone works together, even the biggest and toughest problems can start to get better! Teamwork is powerful! There is always hope when brave people keep trying to help others. Our friends in DR Congo are strong, and they have amazing helpers by their side.

What Can We Do?

Even though we might be far away, we can still think about our friends in DR Congo. We can hope for peace so that the fighting stops. When the fighting stops, it will be much easier for the helpers to do their job and make everyone healthy again. We can also appreciate the brave doctors and nurses who help others every day, especially in difficult places. They are truly heroes! And we can remember to wash our hands often to stay healthy ourselves, just like they tell people in DR Congo! It's a good habit for everyone. Let's send big, warm thoughts and hopes for peace and health to all our friends in DR Congo. Together, we can hope for a brighter, healthier future for them! Stay tuned for more kid-friendly news from Africa!
summary: Brave doctors and health helpers are working hard to stop a serious sickness called Ebola in DR Congo, but ongoing fighting in the country makes it very difficult for them to reach everyone who needs care.
whyMatters: This matters because everyone deserves to be healthy and safe, and the fighting makes it harder for good people to help sick children and grown-ups in DR Congo get better. We need peace so the helpers can do their important work and keep everyone safe from sickness.

originalTitle: US and Iran reach tentative deal for 60-day truce extension, officials say
title: US and Iran reach tentative deal for 60-day truce extension, officials say
content: The memorandum of understanding, which would enable further negotiations, still requires Donald Trump's final approval.

originalTitle: CNN sues Perplexity, alleging unlawful distribution of copyrighted content
title: CNN sues Perplexity, alleging unlawful distribution of copyrighted content
content: Perplexity unlawfully copied thousands of CNN stories, videos and images to power its products, CNN said in its lawsuit.

originalTitle: How War Changed Eid in Lebanon
title: How War Changed Eid in Lebanon
content: After nearly three years of war and displacement, Eid in Lebanon no longer feels like celebration for many families.

title: Gaza City hospitals say several killed in strike, as Israel targets Hamas leaders
content: At least five children were reportedly killed in the strike, which appears to have targeted a Hamas commander.

title: Are US and Iran close to peace or sliding back to war?
content: Neither side seems interested in a return to all-out conflict, despite the latest exchange of strikes.

title: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes
content: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.

originalTitle: Covid era gave Arteta space to revive Arsenal, says Kroenke
title: Covid era gave Arteta space to revive Arsenal, says Kroenke
content: Arsenal co-chair Josh Kroenke says behind-closed-doors football during the Covid-19 pandemic allowed manager Mikel Arteta "space" help revive the "sleeping giant".

originalTitle: Spurs closing in on Bournemouth's Senesi
title: Spurs closing in on Bournemouth's Senesi

title: Clarke signs on until 2030 World Cup - a gamble SFA had to take?
